folio.label: 167r
folio.l:
amyned ©oure cou~seil , in the forseide manere , ne in due manere
as the cas requyretú

¢ ©e han erred also , for ©e han maked , no di-
uisiou~ , bitwixe ©oure cou~seilloås , this is to seyn ; bitwixe ©oure
trewe frendes , & ©oure feyned cou~seilloås ,

ne ©e han nat knowe , æe
wil of ©ourö trewe frendes , olde & wyse ,

but ©e han cast alle here
wordes in an hochepot , And enclyned ©oure herte , to the more
part , & to the grettere noumbre , & there be ©e co~descended ,

And
sithe ©e wot wel , æ men shuln alwey fynde , a grettere nom-
bre of fooles , than of wyse men ,

And æäfore æe cou~seilles æ ben
at co~gregacions & multitudes of folk , there as men take more
reward to the noumbre , than to the sapience of ¹sones ,

©e
se wel æ in swicú cou~seillynggã , fooles han the maistrie

¢ Melibeus answerde a geyn , & seide , I gunte wel æ I ha-
ue erred ,

but there as æè hast told me heere biforn~ , æ he nys
nat to blame , æ chaungetú his cou~seilloås in certeyn cas , &
for certeyn iuste causes

¢ I am al redy to chau~ge my cou~seil-
lours , rigút as æè wolt deuyse

¢ The ¸uäbe seitú , For to do
synne , is mannyssú interlinear gloss .i. humanu~/ODR/, but certes for to ¹seuere longe in sy~-
ne , is werke of the deuele

¢ To this sentence , answered a noon
Dame Pèdence , and seide ,

Examynetú q¶ she , ©oure cou~seil ,
and lat vs se , the whicú of he~ , han spoken most resonably , and
taugút ©ow best cou~seil ,

And for as muche , as that æe examina-
ciou~ is necessarie , lat vs bigynne , at the Surgiens & at the phi-
siciens , æ first speken in this matere ,

I seye , æ Surgiens and
Phisiciens , han seid ©ow in ©oure cou~seil , discretly , as he~ ougúte

And in here speche , seiden ful wysly , that to the office of he~
ap¹tenetú , to don to euäy wigút , honoå & ¸fyt , & no wygút for
to a noye ,

And after here craft , to don gret diligence , vn to æe
cure of hem , whicú æ thei han in here gouänaunce ,

And sire
rigút as æei han answered wysly & discretly ,

rigút so rede I , æ
æei be heigúly & souereynly gerdoned , for here noble speche ,

And eke
for æei shuldenshullenshuldlen do , the more ententif bisinesse , in the curaciou~ of æi
dougúter deere

¢ For al be it so , æ æei ben ©oure frendes , æäfore
shuln ©e nat suffren , æ æei serue ©ow for nougút ,

but ©e ougúte
rathere gerdone hem , & shewe he~ ©oure largesse

æ in maladies , that a contrie is warisshed , by
another contrie ,

I wolde fayn knowe , how ©e vnderstonde æilke
text , & what is ©ourö sentence

¢ Certes q¶ Melibeus , I vnderston-
de it , in this wyse ,

æ rigút as æei han don me a contrie , ri©t
so shulde I don he~ a nother ,

For rigút as they , han venged he~
oon me , & don me wrongö , rigút so shal I venge me vp oon he~
& don he~ wrongö ,
And tha~ne haue I cured a contrie , by a nother
¢ Lo
